other_observers: Lee Croissant; Glenn Walbek found the bird. A photo that he took of the bird is attached.
specific_location: Lee Croissant's home on Ridgeview Lane in Parker CO
description: A bird of typical towhee shape and size. It had a reddish brown cap. Grayish/brown face with flecks of a lighter buffy brown on the cheeks and lores. The throat area was buffy brown with vertical streaks forming a slight necklace. The back of the neck, back, shoulders, upper wings and upper tail coverts were a uniform grayish brown. The upper tail was a dark gray with white tips on the outer two tail feathers. The belly was lighter and the undertail coverts were a warm cinnamon color. The bird was coming to a feeder at a house.Behaviors: The bird would come in to feed around a bird feeder. It came in low to the ground and would typically scratch on the ground in and around the feeder. It did fly up on the feeder briefly then back to the ground.Call: NonePlumage: Basic
